posts.text:
The El Niño climate pattern that is strengthening in the Pacific Ocean is on track to be the largest one in at least 150 years, researchers said today at a briefing.

go.nature.com/4fkayak
posts.external_title: This El Niño is set to be the largest on record by a ‘mind-blowing margin’
posts.external_description: Forecasters predict that the monster climate pattern will combine with global warming to push global temperatures in 2027 to new heights.

posts.text:
Nature research paper: Non-epitaxial perovskite polariton laser diode operating under direct current

go.nature.com/4vIS7Rq
posts.external_title: Non-epitaxial perovskite polariton laser diode operating under direct current - Nature
posts.external_description: By integrating a solution-grown perovskite microplate with chemically inert single-walled carbon nanotube electrodes and embedding them into an optical microcavity, the new microdevices can achieve polariton lasing under a constant direct current at 8 K.

posts.external_title: European summer drying largely driven by atmospheric circulation changes since the 1980s - Nature Geoscience
posts.external_description: Shifting atmospheric circulation patterns over Europe since the 1980s have been the major cause of increasingly severe droughts on the continent, according to nudged circulation climate model simulati...

posts.text:
What does it mean to be an ecologist? 🔎🔬📸

The Ecologists in Action category of our Capturing Ecology competition celebrates the people behind ecology, working to understand and protect our natural world.

Submit your photos by 31 August 👇 BES members enter for free.
posts.external_title: Ecology Photo Competition | Capturing Ecology 2026 | BES
posts.external_description: Enter the BES 2026 annual ecology photo competition and submit pictures of ecology that showcase nature, wildlife and research for a chance to win prizes.

posts.text:
New Editorial: "Europe’s two tiers of genome editing" rdcu.be/fu1Ur

Nature Plants has existed for 11.5 years, during which time the European Union has been constantly struggling towards a clear stance on genome engineering. Perhaps they have finally found it. #PlantScience

News & Views on this paper by Chris Mays (@nhmwien.bsky.social):

www.nature.com/articles/s41...

posts.external_description: Most major mass extinctions in Earth history are linked to greenhouse gas emissions causing warmer and drier climates. Research shows these perturbations also led to widespread wildfires and the expan...

posts.external_description: This analysis shows that a widely used satellite-based ecosystem resilience indicator is a real signal linked to plant physiological responses to water deficit, provided that the estimation is based o...

posts.text:
Registration and call for abstracts for the BES Annual Meeting 2026 are now open 🔥

Our sold-out and Europe's largest ecology conference is returning this year to Birmingham's ICC on 14–17 December, and we can't wait to see you there.

Find out more here 👇
https://tinyurl.com/2s3nupvu
